summary: plugin/run.sh could not be physically deleted from the integrated bundle in the cowork sandbox (delete permission unavailable in unsupervised mode). Reduced to inert comment-only content; integration/manifest.json no longer lists it.
decision: Briefing instructed integrator to delete plugin/run.sh as a vestige of the dropped macOS branch. The cowork sandbox returned 'Operation not permitted' on rm. The allow_cowork_file_delete tool requires user interaction and is unavailable in unsupervised mode. Mitigation: overwrote run.sh contents with comments only (no shebang, no executable code), and removed it from output/integration/manifest.json's files list. On a real Windows StreamDock host, this file is unreferenced (manifest declares CodePathWin pointing at plugin/main.js; CodePathMac is absent) so the host will ignore it. Functionally equivalent to deletion for the v3 deliverable's behavior.
nested_equivalent: Overseer would surface this as an environment constraint, not a design change; Coordinator would approve the comment-only reduction and document for the eventual final-delivery step (which may have delete permission to physically remove it before shipping).
contract_impact: None. CodePathMac and run_sh_contract clauses in C-S1-S3 were already noted as non-load-bearing in impact-analysis-v3 (entry-path rename to CodePathWin->main.js is what's load-bearing).
followup_for_final_delivery: When delivering the final artifact, attempt to physically remove plugin/run.sh; if delete still unavailable, document in run-report.md.
